sentence_reviews.text: Linear and generalized linear models predict through a weighted sum of features, optionally passed through a link function chosen for the target distribution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 5 machine-learning basics on linear models and logistic regression; PML, generalized linear models / linear and logistic regression topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The weighted-feature and link-function characterization is standard and supported across the cited textbook topics, but the packet source pointer is broad rather than an exact passage.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Linear models & generalized linear models is to connect Affine transforms & dense layers, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy to downstream capabilities including Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 5 on linear/logistic regression and maximum likelihood; PML supervised learning, GLM, and tabular/classification/regression topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Affine prediction and likelihood/cross-entropy training are supported, but the downstream application list is graph synthesis and is not directly established by the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supervised learning and generalized linear/classification topics; recommender/ranking application connection is not directly evidenced in the packet.
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear models are plausible baselines for recommendation/ranking, but the cited packet sources do not directly support this downstream substrate claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Medical imaging & clinical decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning and PML supervised classification/regression material; no direct medical-imaging or clinical decision support passage in packet references.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim is plausible historically for clinical risk models, but the packet citations are too general to support the named downstream domain.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML and Deep Learning supervised regression/classification topics for structured feature vectors.
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear/GLM models are directly tied to supervised tabular-style feature prediction, though 'decision support' is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 5 on capacity/generalization/optimization and linear/logistic models; PML model selection and GLM topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency list is supported, but the limitation wording is generic atlas language and not directly sourced for linear/GLM models.

sentence_reviews.text: Linear models & generalized linear models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, linear models and affine/dense transformations; PML linear predictors / GLM topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The affine weighted-sum component is central; the exact component-node wording is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Linear models & generalized linear models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 5 on maximum likelihood and logistic regression / cross-entropy; PML GLM likelihood topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Likelihood and cross-entropy objectives are supported for GLM/logistic cases, though not every linear model uses cross-entropy.

sentence_reviews.text: Linear models & generalized linear models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning and PML linear regression / supervised regression loss material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Regression losses are common for linear regression but not universal across GLMs, so common strength is appropriate.

sentence_reviews.text: Linear models & generalized linear models follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 5 supervised learning examples; PML supervised regression/classification topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear regression and classification are standard supervised-learning models.

edge_reviews.notes: Linear/GLM predictors rely on affine weighted feature combinations; composes/core is defensible.
Maximum-likelihood and cross-entropy objectives are central for GLMs and logistic regression; core is defensible for the combined node.
Regression losses support linear regression but not all GLM use cases; common strength is supported.
Linear and generalized linear models are standard supervised predictors; trained_under/common is supported.
